{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] {{Shrine |Name = Raiden Shrine |image = [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|Main shrine (Okusha and auxiliary shrines are behind the precinct)]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Itakura]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Itakura]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]]

To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] {{Shrine |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|Main shrine (Okusha and auxiliary shrines are behind the precinct)]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing page creation to myself as per first edit summary
revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] {{Shrine |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|Main shrine (Okusha and auxiliary shrines are behind the precinct)]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Raiden revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing page creation to myself as per first edit summary
revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] {{Shrine |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|Main shrine (Okusha and auxiliary shrines are behind the precinct)]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Raiden Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Raiden Shrine revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] {{Shrine |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|Main shrine (Okusha and auxiliary shrines are behind the precinct)]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Unsorted completion Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Unsorted [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] {{Shrine |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|Main shrine (Okusha and auxiliary shrines are behind the precinct)]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)-qq]] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)-qq]] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)-qq]] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|Main shrine (Okusha and auxiliary shrines are behind the precinct)]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)-qq]] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Unsorted [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|Main shrine (Okusha and auxiliary shrines are behind the precinct)]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)-qq]] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)-qq]] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] revision.text:
[[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|Main shrine (Okusha and auxiliary shrines are behind the precinct)]] |Address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Takami-musubi|ja|高産巣日神}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)-qq]] [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]]
