Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Itakura]] [[Category:Buildings in Itakura]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[sn:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[sn:aiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[en:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "rank" to "Rank"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|Main shrine (Okusha and auxiliary shrines are behind the precinct)]] |address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=draft|12=draft}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方|12=draft|12=draft}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神|12=draft|12=draft}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市|12=draft|12=draft}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|12=draft|12=draft}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|12=draft|12=draft}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|12=draft|12=draft}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉|12=draft|12=draft}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft|12=draft}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|12=draft|12=draft}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎|12=draft|12=draft}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Itakura]] [[Category:Buildings in Itakura]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[sn:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[sn:aiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[en:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)}}

Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Itakura]] [[Category:Buildings in Itakura]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[sn:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[sn:aiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[en:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)}}

revision.comment: 42 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:雷電神社 (板倉町) up to rev 103054257
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 雷電神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|本社社殿（奥社と末社は境内裏手）]] |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[邑楽郡]][[板倉町]]板倉2334 | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 13 |緯度秒 = 39.29 | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 36 |経度秒 = 28.06 |祭神 = 火雷大神
大雷大神
別雷大神 |神体 = |社格 = 旧郷社 |創建 = （伝）[[推古天皇]]6年（[[598年]]） |本殿 =二間社[[流造]] |札所等 = |例祭 = 5月1日～5日 |別名=板倉雷電神社 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Gunma }} [[ファイル:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha Haiden 1.JPG|thumb|right|250px|本社社殿（2012年8月）]] [[ファイル:Itakura Raiden Shrine 1.JPG|thumb|right|250px|別棟（2012年8月）]] '''雷電神社'''（らいでんじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[邑楽郡]][[板倉町]]板倉にある[[神社]]。主に[[関東地方]]に点在する「雷電神社」「雷電社」の事実上の総本社格であるが、他の雷電神社と区別する意味で'''板倉雷電神社'''（いたくららいでんじんじゃ）とも呼ばれる。[[茨城県]][[水戸市]]の別雷皇太神、同県[[つくば市]]の[[金村別雷神社]]と並ぶ関東三雷神の1社とされるつくば市教育委員会 編（2009）：12ページ。 == 概要 == 『雲間山（はさまさん）竜蔵寺縁起』によれば、[[推古天皇]]6年（[[598年]]）、[[聖徳太子]]の創建とされる{{Sfn|板倉町史編さん委員会|1985|pp=664-665,752}}{{Sfn|群馬県邑楽郡教育会|1917|pp=279-291}}。 [[延暦]]年間（[[782年]] - [[806年]]）に[[坂上田村麻呂]]が社殿を造営したとされ、『[[上野国神名帳]]』にみえる邑楽郡「従四位上 火雷神社」は当社であるとされている{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |last=尾崎 |author=尾崎喜左雄 |first=喜左雄 |author-link=尾崎喜左雄 |doi=10.11501/12266236 |page=348}}{{要登録}}{{Sfn|板倉町史編さん委員会|1985|pp=664-665,752}}{{Sfn|群馬県邑楽郡教育会|1917|pp=279-291}}。[[天文 (元号)|天文]]16年（[[1547年]]）に佐貫庄の[[上野赤井氏|赤井氏]]旗下、篠崎三河守が全神殿を造営し、[[元亀]]4年（[[1573年]]）にも[[長尾顕長]]が造営を行った{{Sfn|板倉町史編さん委員会|1985|pp=664-665}}{{Sfn|群馬県邑楽郡教育会|1917|pp=279-291}}{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|p=380}}。 古くから[[雷]]の被害が多い土地であり、また暴れ川で度々流路を変えた[[渡良瀬川]]と[[利根川]]との間にあって度々[[火災]]や[[水害]]に見舞われたが、[[延宝]]2年（[[1674年]]）、当地を治めていた[[館林藩]]の[[藩主]]であった[[徳川綱吉]]が社殿を再建し（社殿に徳川家の[[葵紋]]を使うことを許された{{Sfn|板倉町史編さん委員会|1985|pp=664-665}}）、後に彼が長じて[[江戸幕府]]の第5代[[征夷大将軍|将軍]]となるに及んで、次第に繁栄するようになった。現在の社殿は[[天保]]6年（[[1835年]]）、奥社社殿は[[慶応]]4年（[[1868年]]）の造営である。社殿には{{要出典範囲|[[左甚五郎]]から10代目の|date=2024年8月}}石原常八の手による彫刻が廻らされている{{Sfn|板倉町史編さん委員会|1985|p=758}}。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[郷社]]に列せられる{{Sfn|板倉町史編さん委員会|1985|pp=664-665}}{{Sfn|群馬県邑楽郡教育会|1917|pp=279-291}}。[[第二次世界大戦]]後に宗教法人化された。今日では雷除けはもとより、氷嵐除けや豊作祈願、厄除けや安全、特に[[電気]]関係をはじめとする諸工事の安全の神として名高い。また、毎年[[大型連休]]中の例祭や、春の[[ロウバイ|蝋梅]]および[[椿]]が美しい神社としても名高い。例祭以外にも主に[[企業]]関係者による安全祈願や商売繁盛の[[祈祷]]の依頼が多く、5月中旬から初秋までの[[雷雨]]の季節を中心に頻繁に行われている。 神社境内の一部及び隣接する山林地域は群馬県緑地環境保全地域となっている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.gunma.jp/page/7125.html|title=雷電神社（広域図）|publisher=群馬県|date=|accessdate=2024-12-24}}。 == 祭神 == 主祭神 * [[火雷大神]]（ほのいかづちのおおかみ） * 大雷大神（おおいかづちのおおかみ） * [[別雷神|別雷大神]]（わけいかづちのおおかみ） 相殿祭神 * [[伊邪那美命]] * [[水分神|天水分命]] * [[天之御中主神|天御中主神]] * [[タカミムスビ|高産巣日神]] * [[カミムスビ|神産巣日神]] * [[天照大神|大日孁神]] * [[建角身神]] * [[菅原道真]]公 * [[倉稲魂命]] * [[大山咋命]] * [[ミヅハノメ|弥都波売神]] * 天神 * 地祇 末社・八幡宮稲荷神社社殿祭神 * [[誉田別尊]] * 倉稲魂神 {{Sfn|群馬県邑楽郡教育会|1917|pp=279-291}}{{Sfn|板倉町史編さん委員会|1985|pp=664-665,752}} == 当社にまつわる伝承 == {{出典の明記| date = 2024年8月| section = 1}} * 延暦20年（[[801年]]）、東征した坂上田村麻呂が社殿を造営したところ、その宮祭の夜、境内にあった[[杉]]の木の梢に沼の中から灯が上ってきて闇を照らした、とされる。現在境内東側にて枯れて幹の一部から下のみを残す「龍灯の杉」に纏わる伝説として語り継がれる。 * 本社社殿裏手にある奥社には伊邪那美大神（いざなみのおおかみ）が祀られ、家内円満・子授け・安産・縁結びなどを叶える女神とされている。 * 境内裏手の別棟に置かれている[[ナマズ|鯰（ナマズ）]]の石像は「なまずさん」と呼ばれ、撫でると[[地震]]を除け、元気回復・視力改善・自信が湧き出る、などのご利益があると云われる。また、門前では鯰料理が名物となっている。 * 境内にある椿の庭には[[福禄寿]]（ふくろくじゅ）の石像があり、3つの幸せ（子孫繁栄・財産富裕・不老長寿）を授けるとされる。 == 郷土料理 == [[郷土料理]]として、[[ナマズ料理]]が有名。 == 文化財 == ===重要文化財=== * 末社八幡宮稲荷神社社殿{{Cite web |title=雷電神社(末社八幡宮・稲荷神社社殿) - 板倉町 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |website=www.town.itakura.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-28}} - [[室町時代]]後期（[[1547年]]）の建立。二間社[[入母屋造]]、[[向拝]]二間、銅板葺。二間社の社殿に2柱を祀る珍しい形式[[中嶋神社]]本殿（重要文化財）など7例が確認されている。。明治41年（[[1908年]]）8月1日に[[古社寺保存法]]により特別保護建造物に指定され、文化財保護法施行に伴い[[昭和]]25年（[[1950年]]）8月29日に旧国宝から[[重要文化財]]に指定替えとなった。昭和35年（[[1960年]]）解体修理に伴い[[切妻造]]桟瓦葺を入母屋造銅板葺に改めた。神社建築としては群馬県最古{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-3-30 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |editor=板倉町史編さん委員会 |doi=10.11501/9643464 |volume=上巻 |pages=536-545}}{{要登録}}{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|p=385}}。 ===群馬県指定重要文化財=== * 雷電神社社殿（本殿・幣殿・拝殿・奥宮） - 本殿は二間社流造[[破風|千鳥破風]]付瓦棒銅板葺で幣殿とともに天保6年（1835年）の建築。拝殿は[[文政]]2年（[[1819年]]）の建築で[[入母屋造]]平入り千鳥破風付向拝唐破風付瓦棒銅板葺。奥宮は慶応4年（1868年）の建築で三間社入母屋造平入り、瓦棒銅板葺{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=381-384}}。 === 板倉町指定重要文化財 === * 雷電神社棟札{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|p=380}} * 高瀬舟絵馬{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|p=380}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-2-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |ref=harv |editor=板倉町史編さん委員会 |doi=10.11501/9643465 |volume=下巻}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |ref=harv |author=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |doi=10.11501/951646}} * {{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |ref=harv}} * つくば市教育委員会 編『つくば市の文化財 2009年版』つくば市、2009年、125pp. == 関連項目 == * [[雷電神社]] * [[神社一覧]] * [[脚折雨乞]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp 雷電神社] {{DEFAULTSORT:らいてんしんしやいたくらまち}} [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:群馬県の重要文化財]] [[Category:群馬県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:板倉町の建築物]] [[Category:板倉町の歴史]] [[Category:雷電神社|いたくらまち]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

大雷大神

1.JPG|thumb|right|250px|本社社殿（2012年8月）]] [[ファイル:Itakura Raiden Shrine 1.JPG|thumb|right|250px|別棟（2012年8月）]] '''雷電神社'''（らいでんじんじゃ）は、[[群馬県]][[邑楽郡]][[板倉町]]板倉にある[[神社]]。主に[[関東地方]]に点在する「雷電神社」「雷電社」の事実上の総本社格であるが、他の雷電神社と区別する意味で'''板倉雷電神社'''（いたくららいでんじんじゃ）とも呼ばれる。[[茨城県]][[水戸市]]の別雷皇太神、同県[[つくば市]]の[[金村別雷神社]]と並ぶ関東三雷神の1社とされるつくば市教育委員会 編（2009）：12ページ。 == 概要 == 『雲間山（はさまさん）竜蔵寺縁起』によれば、[[推古天皇]]6年（[[598年]]）、[[聖徳太子]]の創建とされる{{Sfn|板倉町史編さん委員会|1985|pp=664-665,752}}{{Sfn|群馬県邑楽郡教育会|1917|pp=279-291}}。 [[延暦]]年間（[[782年]] - [[806年]]）に[[坂上田村麻呂]]が社殿を造営したとされ、『[[上野国神名帳]]』にみえる邑楽郡「従四位上 火雷神社」は当社であるとされている{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |last=尾崎 |author=尾崎喜左雄 |first=喜左雄 |author-link=尾崎喜左雄 |doi=10.11501/12266236 |page=348}}{{要登録}}{{Sfn|板倉町史編さん委員会|1985|pp=664-665,752}}{{Sfn|群馬県邑楽郡教育会|1917|pp=279-291}}。[[天文 (元号)|天文]]16年（[[1547年]]）に佐貫庄の[[上野赤井氏|赤井氏]]旗下、篠崎三河守が全神殿を造営し、[[元亀]]4年（[[1573年]]）にも[[長尾顕長]]が造営を行った{{Sfn|板倉町史編さん委員会|1985|pp=664-665}}{{Sfn|群馬県邑楽郡教育会|1917|pp=279-291}}{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|p=380}}。 古くから[[雷]]の被害が多い土地であり、また暴れ川で度々流路を変えた[[渡良瀬川]]と[[利根川]]との間にあって度々[[火災]]や[[水害]]に見舞われたが、[[延宝]]2年（[[1674年]]）、当地を治めていた[[館林藩]]の[[藩主]]であった[[徳川綱吉]]が社殿を再建し（社殿に徳川家の[[葵紋]]を使うことを許された{{Sfn|板倉町史編さん委員会|1985|pp=664-665}}）、後に彼が長じて[[江戸幕府]]の第5代[[征夷大将軍|将軍]]となるに及んで、次第に繁栄するようになった。現在の社殿は[[天保]]6年（[[1835年]]）、奥社社殿は[[慶応]]4年（[[1868年]]）の造営である。社殿には{{要出典範囲|[[左甚五郎]]から10代目の|date=2024年8月}}石原常八の手による彫刻が廻らされている{{Sfn|板倉町史編さん委員会|1985|p=758}}。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[郷社]]に列せられる{{Sfn|板倉町史編さん委員会|1985|pp=664-665}}{{Sfn|群馬県邑楽郡教育会|1917|pp=279-291}}。[[第二次世界大戦]]後に宗教法人化された。今日では雷除けはもとより、氷嵐除けや豊作祈願、厄除けや安全、特に[[電気]]関係をはじめとする諸工事の安全の神として名高い。また、毎年[[大型連休]]中の例祭や、春の[[ロウバイ|蝋梅]]および[[椿]]が美しい神社としても名高い。例祭以外にも主に[[企業]]関係者による安全祈願や商売繁盛の[[祈祷]]の依頼が多く、5月中旬から初秋までの[[雷雨]]の季節を中心に頻繁に行われている。 神社境内の一部及び隣接する山林地域は群馬県緑地環境保全地域となっている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.gunma.jp/page/7125.html|title=雷電神社（広域図）|publisher=群馬県|date=|accessdate=2024-12-24}}。 == 祭神 == 主祭神 * [[火雷大神]]（ほのいかづちのおおかみ） * 大雷大神（おおいかづちのおおかみ） * [[別雷神|別雷大神]]（わけいかづちのおおかみ） 相殿祭神 * [[伊邪那美命]] * [[水分神|天水分命]] * [[天之御中主神|天御中主神]] * [[タカミムスビ|高産巣日神]] * [[カミムスビ|神産巣日神]] * [[天照大神|大日孁神]] * [[建角身神]] * [[菅原道真]]公 * [[倉稲魂命]] * [[大山咋命]] * [[ミヅハノメ|弥都波売神]] * 天神 * 地祇 末社・八幡宮稲荷神社社殿祭神 * [[誉田別尊]] * 倉稲魂神 {{Sfn|群馬県邑楽郡教育会|1917|pp=279-291}}{{Sfn|板倉町史編さん委員会|1985|pp=664-665,752}} == 当社にまつわる伝承 == {{出典の明記| date = 2024年8月| section = 1}} * 延暦20年（[[801年]]）、東征した坂上田村麻呂が社殿を造営したところ、その宮祭の夜、境内にあった[[杉]]の木の梢に沼の中から灯が上ってきて闇を照らした、とされる。現在境内東側にて枯れて幹の一部から下のみを残す「龍灯の杉」に纏わる伝説として語り継がれる。 * 本社社殿裏手にある奥社には伊邪那美大神（いざなみのおおかみ）が祀られ、家内円満・子授け・安産・縁結びなどを叶える女神とされている。 * 境内裏手の別棟に置かれている[[ナマズ|鯰（ナマズ）]]の石像は「なまずさん」と呼ばれ、撫でると[[地震]]を除け、元気回復・視力改善・自信が湧き出る、などのご利益があると云われる。また、門前では鯰料理が名物となっている。 * 境内にある椿の庭には[[福禄寿]]（ふくろくじゅ）の石像があり、3つの幸せ（子孫繁栄・財産富裕・不老長寿）を授けるとされる。 == 郷土料理 == [[郷土料理]]として、[[ナマズ料理]]が有名。 == 文化財 == ===重要文化財=== * 末社八幡宮稲荷神社社殿{{Cite web |title=雷電神社(末社八幡宮・稲荷神社社殿) - 板倉町 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |website=www.town.itakura.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-08-28}} - [[室町時代]]後期（[[1547年]]）の建立。二間社[[入母屋造]]、[[向拝]]二間、銅板葺。二間社の社殿に2柱を祀る珍しい形式[[中嶋神社]]本殿（重要文化財）など7例が確認されている。。明治41年（[[1908年]]）8月1日に[[古社寺保存法]]により特別保護建造物に指定され、文化財保護法施行に伴い[[昭和]]25年（[[1950年]]）8月29日に旧国宝から[[重要文化財]]に指定替えとなった。昭和35年（[[1960年]]）解体修理に伴い[[切妻造]]桟瓦葺を入母屋造銅板葺に改めた。神社建築としては群馬県最古{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-3-30 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |editor=板倉町史編さん委員会 |doi=10.11501/9643464 |volume=上巻 |pages=536-545}}{{要登録}}{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|p=385}}。 ===群馬県指定重要文化財=== * 雷電神社社殿（本殿・幣殿・拝殿・奥宮） - 本殿は二間社流造[[破風|千鳥破風]]付瓦棒銅板葺で幣殿とともに天保6年（1835年）の建築。拝殿は[[文政]]2年（[[1819年]]）の建築で[[入母屋造]]平入り千鳥破風付向拝唐破風付瓦棒銅板葺。奥宮は慶応4年（1868年）の建築で三間社入母屋造平入り、瓦棒銅板葺{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|pp=381-384}}。 === 板倉町指定重要文化財 === * 雷電神社棟札{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|p=380}} * 高瀬舟絵馬{{Sfn|群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課|2022|p=380}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-2-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |ref=harv |editor=板倉町史編さん委員会 |doi=10.11501/9643465 |volume=下巻}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |ref=harv |author=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |doi=10.11501/951646}} * {{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |ref=harv}} * つくば市教育委員会 編『つくば市の文化財 2009年版』つくば市、2009年、125pp. == 関連項目 == * [[雷電神社]] * [[神社一覧]] * [[脚折雨乞]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp 雷電神社] {{DEFAULTSORT:らいてんしんしやいたくらまち}} [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:群馬県の重要文化財]] [[Category:群馬県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:板倉町の建築物]] [[Category:板倉町の歴史]] [[Category:雷電神社|いたくらまち]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|Main shrine (Okusha and auxiliary shrines are behind the precinct)]] |address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Itakura]] [[Category:Buildings in Itakura]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[sn:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[sn:aiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[en:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)}} {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Itakura]] [[Category:Buildings in Itakura]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[sn:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[sn:aiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[en:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|Main shrine (Okusha and auxiliary shrines are behind the precinct)]] |address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former Gōsha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Itakura]] [[Category:Buildings in Itakura]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[sn:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[sn:aiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[en:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]]

His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Itakura]] [[Category:Buildings in Itakura]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[sn:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[sn:aiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[en:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|Main shrine (Okusha and auxiliary shrines are behind the precinct)]] |address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Itakura]] [[Category:Buildings in Itakura]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[sn:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[sn:aiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[en:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]]

It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Itakura]] [[Category:Buildings in Itakura]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[sn:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[sn:aiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[en:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]]

To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku era|qq=}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|qq=}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|qq=}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō|qq=}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidari Jingorō|qq=}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusanoikazuchi|qq=}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|qq=}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Itakura]] [[Category:Buildings in Itakura]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[sn:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[sn:aiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[en:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|Main shrine (Okusha and auxiliary shrines are behind the precinct)]] |address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakura|qq=}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinokazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|qq=}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko|qq=}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itakura|qq=}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō region|qq=}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Thunder Shrines|qq=}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai Kōtaijin|qq=}} in {{ill|Mito|ja|水戸市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|ja|金村別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|ja|つくば市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuba|qq=}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko|qq=}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōtoku|qq=}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku era|ja|延暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku era|qq=}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|qq=}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|ja|渡良瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarase River|qq=}} and {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tone River|qq=}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|ja|徳川綱吉|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|qq=}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō|qq=}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidari Jingorō|qq=}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|lt=Hinokazuchi|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusanoikazuchi|qq=}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|qq=}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Itakura]] [[Category:Buildings in Itakura]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[sn:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[sn:aiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[en:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]]

The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby [[:en:Watarase River|Watarase River]] and [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]]. In 1674 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 2), the local lord, [[:en:Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]], rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]] (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|qq=}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]). revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG|250px|Main shrine (Okusha and auxiliary shrines are behind the precinct)]] |address = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Itakura, Gunma|Itakura]], Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]]
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|qq=}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = Traditionally [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in [[:en:Itakura, Gunma|Itakura]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]]. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Thunder Shrines|qq=}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai Kōtaijin|qq=}} in [[:en:Mito, Ibaraki|Mito]] and [[:en:Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsukuba|Tsukuba]]Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 6 (598) under the direction of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] (782–806), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby [[:en:Watarase River|Watarase River]] and [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]]. In 1674 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 2), the local lord, [[:en:Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]], rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]], adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]] (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|qq=}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|qq=}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties]] [[Category:History of Itakura]] [[Category:Buildings in Itakura]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[sn:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[sn:aiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[en:Raiden Shrine (Itakura, Gunma)]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]]
