Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]] (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Architecture of Itakura Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itakura]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Raiden Shrines]] {{Commons category|Raiden-jinja (Itakura)}} {{wikidata link|Q11660224}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660224]] Wikidata name: Raiden Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG |address = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Itakura, Gunma|Itakura]], Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]]
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = Traditionally [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma |location = {{ill|Itakura|WD=Q1203771}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.22752777777778|N|139.60783333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in [[:en:Itakura, Gunma|Itakura]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]]. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Mito, Ibaraki|Mito]] and [[:en:Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsukuba|Tsukuba]]Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 6 (598) under the direction of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] (782–806), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby [[:en:Watarase River|Watarase River]] and [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]]. In 1674 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 2), the local lord, [[:en:Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]], rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]], adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]] (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Architecture of Itakura Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itakura]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Raiden Shrines]] {{Commons category|Raiden-jinja (Itakura)}} {{wikidata link|Q11660224}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660224]] Wikidata name: Raiden Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG |address = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Itakura, Gunma|Itakura]], Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]]
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = Traditionally [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma |location = {{ill|Itakura|WD=Q1203771}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.22752777777778|N|139.60783333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in [[:en:Itakura, Gunma|Itakura]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]]. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Mito, Ibaraki|Mito]] and [[:en:Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsukuba|Tsukuba]]Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 6 (598) under the direction of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] (782–806), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby [[:en:Watarase River|Watarase River]] and [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]]. In 1674 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 2), the local lord, [[:en:Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]], rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]], adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]] (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Architecture of Itakura Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itakura]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Raiden Shrines]] {{Commons category|Raiden-jinja (Itakura)}} Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]] (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Architecture of Itakura Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itakura]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Raiden Shrines]] {{Commons category|Raiden-jinja (Itakura)}} {{wikidata link|Q11660224}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660224]] Wikidata name: Raiden Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG |address = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Itakura, Gunma|Itakura]], Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]]
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = Traditionally [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma |location = {{ill|Itakura|WD=Q1203771}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.22752777777778|N|139.60783333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in [[:en:Itakura, Gunma|Itakura]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]]. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Mito, Ibaraki|Mito]] and [[:en:Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsukuba|Tsukuba]]Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 6 (598) under the direction of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] (782–806), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby [[:en:Watarase River|Watarase River]] and [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]]. In 1674 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 2), the local lord, [[:en:Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]], rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]], adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]] (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Architecture of Itakura Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itakura]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Raiden Shrines]] {{Commons category|Raiden-jinja (Itakura)}} {{wikidata link|Q11660224}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660224]] Wikidata name: Raiden Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG |address = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Itakura, Gunma|Itakura]], Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]]
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = Traditionally [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma |location = {{ill|Itakura|WD=Q1203771}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.22752777777778|N|139.60783333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in [[:en:Itakura, Gunma|Itakura]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]]. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Mito, Ibaraki|Mito]] and [[:en:Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsukuba|Tsukuba]]Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 6 (598) under the direction of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] (782–806), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby [[:en:Watarase River|Watarase River]] and [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]]. In 1674 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 2), the local lord, [[:en:Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]], rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]], adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]] (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] {{Commons category|Raiden-jinja (Itakura)}} {{wikidata link|Q11660224}} [[Category:Architecture of Itakura Town]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itakura]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Raiden Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]] (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] {{Commons category|Raiden-jinja (Itakura)}} {{wikidata link|Q11660224}} [[Category:Architecture of Itakura Town]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itakura]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Raiden Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660224]] Wikidata name: Raiden Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG |address = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], [[:en:Itakura, Gunma|Itakura]], Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]]
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = Traditionally [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma |location = {{ill|Itakura|WD=Q1203771}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.22752777777778|N|139.60783333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in [[:en:Itakura, Gunma|Itakura]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]]. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Mito, Ibaraki|Mito]] and [[:en:Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine]] in [[:en:Tsukuba|Tsukuba]]Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 6 (598) under the direction of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]]{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku era]] (782–806), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby [[:en:Watarase River|Watarase River]] and [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]]. In 1674 ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 2), the local lord, [[:en:Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi]], rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]], adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Hinokazuchi]] (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] {{Commons category|Raiden-jinja (Itakura)}} {{wikidata link|Q11660224}} [[Category:Architecture of Itakura Town]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itakura]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Raiden Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 4). Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Hinokazuchi}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] {{Commons category|Raiden-jinja (Itakura)}} {{wikidata link|Q11660224}} [[Category:Architecture of Itakura Town]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itakura]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Raiden Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660224]] Wikidata name: Raiden Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG |address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|en|Itakura, Gunma|WD=Q1203771|lt=Itakura}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Hinokazuchi}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Suiko}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma |location = {{ill|Itakura|WD=Q1203771}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.22752777777778|N|139.60783333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|en|Itakura, Gunma|WD=Q1203771|lt=Itakura}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Mito, Ibaraki|en|Mito, Ibaraki|WD=Q204249|lt=Mito}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|en|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|WD=Q11647233|lt=Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|en|Tsukuba|WD=Q273863|lt=Tsukuba}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Suiko}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku era}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|en|Watarase River|WD=Q3566607|lt=Watarase River}} and {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|en|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|WD=Q313745|lt=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|en|Hidari Jingorō|WD=Q2668220|lt=Hidari Jingorō}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Hinokazuchi}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] {{Commons category|Raiden-jinja (Itakura)}} {{wikidata link|Q11660224}} [[Category:Architecture of Itakura Town]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itakura]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Raiden Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Hinokazuchi}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] {{Commons category|Raiden-jinja (Itakura)}} {{wikidata link|Q11660224}} [[Category:Architecture of Itakura Town]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itakura]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Raiden Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Raiden-jinja (Itakura)]][[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Raiden Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Itakura Raiden Shrine Honsha 1.JPG |address = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|en|Itakura, Gunma|WD=Q1203771|lt=Itakura}}, Itakura 2334 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 13 |Latitude seconds = 39.29 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 36 |Longitude seconds = 28.06 |Deities = {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Hinokazuchi}}
{{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = Traditionally {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Suiko}} 6 (598) |Main hall = Two-bay Nagare-zukuri |Alias = Itakura Raiden Shrine |Festivals = May 1–5 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Gunma |location = {{ill|Itakura|WD=Q1203771}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.22752777777778|N|139.60783333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Raiden Shrine''' (雷電神社), located in {{ill|Itakura, Gunma|en|Itakura, Gunma|WD=Q1203771|lt=Itakura}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, is a Shinto shrine considered the de facto head shrine of Raiden Shrines across the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}}. To distinguish it from others, it is also known as '''Itakura Raiden Shrine''' (板倉雷電神社). It is one of the {{ill|Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja|関東三雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Thunder Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Kantō region, alongside {{ill|Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja|別雷皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsurai Kōtaijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Mito, Ibaraki|en|Mito, Ibaraki|WD=Q204249|lt=Mito}} and {{ill|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|en|Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine|WD=Q11647233|lt=Kanamura Wake Ikazuchi Shrine}} in {{ill|Tsukuba|en|Tsukuba|WD=Q273863|lt=Tsukuba}}Tsukuba City Board of Education (2009): p.12. == Overview == According to the 'Hasama-san Ryūzō-ji Engi,' the shrine was founded in {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Suiko}} 6 (598) under the direction of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-02-20 |year=1985 |publisher=板倉町史編さん委員会 |pages=664–665, 752}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=群馬県邑楽郡誌 |date=1917-12-15 |year=1917 |publisher=群馬県邑楽郡教育会 |pages=279–291}}. During the {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku era}} (782–806), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} reportedly constructed the shrine building. It is identified as the "Fire Thunder Shrine of Saku District" listed in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=上野国神名帳の研究 |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=尾崎先生著書刊行会 |page=348}}. The shrine has a history of disasters caused by thunderstorms and the changing courses of the nearby {{ill|Watarase River|en|Watarase River|WD=Q3566607|lt=Watarase River}} and {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}}. In 1674 ({{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō}} 2), the local lord, {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|en|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|WD=Q313745|lt=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi}}, rebuilt the shrine buildings. His later elevation to the fifth {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} shōgun brought prosperity to the shrine. The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 4). Notable carvings by Ishihara Jōhachi, purportedly the 10th-generation successor to {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|en|Hidari Jingorō|WD=Q2668220|lt=Hidari Jingorō}}, adorn the buildings. === Status === * Heian Period – Recorded as "Fire Thunder Shrine" (従四位上 火雷神社) in the {{ill|Jōshū Shinpō|ja|上野国神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshū Shinpō|qq=}}. * 1872 (Meiji 5) – Ranked as Gōsha. * Post-WWII – Registered as a religious corporation. Today, the shrine is renowned for thunder protection, storm prevention, agricultural blessings, and electrical safety. Visitors frequently request prayers for safety, prosperity, and storm protection, particularly during the thunderstorm season from mid-May to early autumn. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Deities === * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Hinokazuchi}} (Honoi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Ōikazuchi|ja|大雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōi-kazuchi-no-Ōkami) * {{ill|Wakeminokazuchi|ja|別雷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakeminokazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Wakeminokazuchi-no-Ōkami) === Additional Deities === * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|弥都波売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Local heavenly and earthly deities == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Auxiliary Shrine Hachiman-Inari Shrine – Built in 1547 (late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}). Two-bay Irimoya-zukuri, unique for housing two deities{{Cite book|和書 |title=板倉町史 |date=1985-03-30 |pages=536–545}}. === Gunma Prefecture Designations === * Raiden Shrine Buildings (Main Hall, Heiden, Haiden, Okusha) * Raiden Shrine Plaques * Takase-bune Ema (votive plaque){{Cite web |title=雷電神社 |url=https://www.town.itakura.gunma.jp/cont/s021000/map/raiden_shisetsu_inari.html |access-date=2024-08-28}}. == Folklore and Traditions == * The "Dragon Lantern Cedar" on the east precinct is tied to a legend of a light rising from the swamp during an ancient festival. * The Okusha honors Izanami, a goddess of family harmony and childbirth. * A stone catfish figure, nicknamed "Namazu-san," is believed to ward off earthquakes and grant confidence. == Local Cuisine == * Famous for catfish-based dishes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.raiden.or.jp Official Website of Raiden Shrine] {{translated page|ja|雷電神社 (板倉町)|version=103054257|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:雷電神社_(板倉町)]] [[ja:雷電神社 (板倉町)]] {{Commons category|Raiden-jinja (Itakura)}} {{wikidata link|Q11660224}} [[Category:Architecture of Itakura Town]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itakura]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Raiden Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The current shrine buildings date back to 1835 ({{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 6), while the Okusha was built in 1868 ({{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 4).
